<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a thorough understanding of the design document. Let me create a comprehensive tasks.json file that follows the user's requirements:

1. Nano-level implementation (纳米级实现) - very granular tasks
2. Each task independently testable, modular design
3. Task status fields for progress tracking

Let me organize the tasks by modules based on the architecture:

The main modules are:
1. Project setup (manifest, folder structure)
2. Background / Service Worker
3. Content Script - early-inject.js
4. Content Script - content.js (DOM tools)
5. Side Panel - UI (panel.html, panel.css, panel.js)


6. Side Panel - Core modules (session.js, profile.js, api.js, css-merge.js, tools.js, agent-loop.js)
7. Storage (chrome.storage + IndexedDB)
8. Skills (static skill files)
9. Error handling
10. Testing

Now I'm breaking this down into very granular nano-level tasks with a structured format that includes task IDs, module assignments, titles, descriptions, and status tracking fields.

For Phase 1, I need to set up the manifest, create the folder structure, and add placeholder icons. Phase 2 focuses on the storage layer—building an IndexedDB wrapper with functions for opening databases and managing history, creating a SessionContext class and StyleSkillStore class, implementing cleanup logic, and handling schema migrations. Phase 3 involves the content scripts: setting up early-inject.js for permanent style injection, creating the message listener skeleton in content.js, implementing the get_domain function, and building out getPageStructure.

Now I'm working through the remaining content script utilities—helper functions for grep implementation, CSS injection via both style elements and adopted stylesheets, CSP detection with auto-dispatch, CSS rollback functionality, and SPA navigation detection. Phase 4 covers the side panel modules: building the API layer with settings CRUD, API permission management, connection validation, streaming calls, and error handling; creating the CSS merge utilities for parsing rules and merging stylesheets; implementing session management with CRUD operations and metadata helpers; adding user profile functionality; and defining the tool configurations.

For Phase 5, I'm building out the side panel UI with the HTML skeleton and base styles, then implementing the various views and components—the first-time guide, settings panel, and main chat interface with its top bar, skill quick access area, message rendering, collapsible tool call cards, action confirmation overlays, input area states, session dropdown, and status indicators.

Now I'm finishing the panel with error banners and streaming text display, then moving into the background service worker setup. After that comes the static skill resources like design principles and color theory guides, followed by wiring everything together with error handling, restricted page detection, and storage cleanup. Finally, I'll need to test across unit tests for the CSS merge and content script functions, integration tests with puppeteer, and a manual test checklist.</details></think>
